name: Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra Loop
name_original: Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra — vuelta desde Villanueva de Campeán
description: Explore the Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra, a moderate 30.5-mile mountain bike route featuring the ancient Fat Cork Oak.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villanueva De Campeán offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills and historical pathways. The region features routes along former railway lines like the Vía Verde de la Plata, providing smoother terrain. Riders can also explore paths near the Duero River and ascend to local hills such as Teso Santo, encountering varied elevations and natural scenery. This area provides a mix of moderate challenges suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Campeán The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata, a 29.5 miles (47.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic greenway, offering a consistent surface for cycling. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.6 km) path. This trail features ascents to Teso Santo and follows a historic route with varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Duero Route and Sogo Bridge Loop from Pereruela, a 21.2 miles (34.2 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Campeán is defined by historical greenways, river valleys, and moderate hills, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Villanueva De Campeán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Campeán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villanueva De Campeán?
A: There are 7 mountain bike trails documented around Villanueva De Campeán, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villanueva De Campeán?
A: While there are no trails explicitly rated as 'easy' in the current selection, many of the routes, particularly those following the Vía Verde de la Plata, offer smoother terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that utilizes a historic greenway, providing a consistent surface that might be more approachable for those building their skills.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: The longest mountain bike trail currently listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende – Wind Turbines on Teso Santo loop from Peñausende&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 38.5 miles (62 km) and is rated as difficult.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike routes around Villanueva De Campeán?
A: Yes, there is one trail rated as difficult: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende – Wind Turbines on Teso Santo loop from Peñausende&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers nearly 38.5 miles (62 km) with significant elevation changes, making it suitable for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that form a loop?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in the region are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282237388/hike-from-penausende-circular-route-via-teso-santo-and-the-contrabandists-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282127585/duero-route-and-sogo-bridge-loop-from-pereruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Route and Sogo Bridge Loop from Pereruela&lt;/a&gt;, all offering circular routes for convenient exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villanueva De Campeán?
A: The terrain around Villanueva De Campeán is diverse, featuring rolling hills and historical pathways. You'll find routes along former railway lines like the Vía Verde de la Plata, which offer smoother surfaces, as well as paths near the Duero River and ascents to local hills such as Teso Santo, providing varied elevations and natural scenery.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Campeán?
A: While exploring the trails, you might encounter various points of interest. Notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4423910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monoliths of the Three Causeways&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument of the Three Pilgrimage Routes&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find scenic viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4420899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Salamanca Plains&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Villanueva De Campeán?
A: The mountain biking routes in Villanueva De Campeán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Riders often praise the blend of historical greenways, river valleys, and moderate hills, which offer varied experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by the Duero River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282127585/duero-route-and-sogo-bridge-loop-from-pereruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Route and Sogo Bridge Loop from Pereruela&lt;/a&gt; is a notable trail that leads through riverine landscapes, offering scenic views along the Duero River.

Q: Are there any trails that ascend to Teso Santo?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282237388/hike-from-penausende-circular-route-via-teso-santo-and-the-contrabandists-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path&lt;/a&gt; features ascents to Teso Santo, providing elevated views and varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villanueva De Campeán?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Vía Verde de la Plata?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that follows this historic greenway, known for its consistent surface and scenic path.
tips: The track is in very good condition and the views it gives you of the plain are impressive. From this point you can see the mosaic of colors offered by the fields of Salamanca from kilometers away.

An authentic living cathedral worth visiting. Near the town of Santiz, we find this hundred-year-old cork oak specimen, which is estimated to be around 600 years old. It is part of the Route of the Living Cathedrals
It is accessed through a metal door that is closed to protect the tree.
Nice picture rolling along a road in good condition near the town of Villanueva de Campeán. From here we have views of the XV century Convent of Santa María del Soto.

faqs.question: Where is the starting point for the Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop that starts and returns from Villanueva de Campeán. You can typically find parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra is classified as a moderate mountain biking trail. While not overly technical, its distance of nearly 50 km and moderate elevation gain mean it's best suited for riders with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is primarily a mountain biking route, suggesting a mix of unpaved roads, gravel paths, and potentially some singletrack. While specific details aren't extensively provided, its moderate rating implies varied surfaces and some gradients, especially given the elevation changes.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the impressive Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra, a centenarian cork oak estimated to be around 600 years old. You'll also encounter a View of the Salamanca Plains and ride along sections of the Vía Verde de la Plata.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Salamanca region, spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this trail. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga. You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with the Cañada Real de Vizana o de la Plata and Camino Lomo Judío.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain trail etiquette.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Ruta del Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra mountain bike route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Villanueva de Campeán, a village where you might find basic amenities. For more options regarding cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, you would likely need to look towards larger towns in the vicinity, such as Calahorra or other nearby communities in the Zamora region.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra?
faqs.answer: The Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra is a remarkable natural monument. This ancient cork oak is estimated to be around 600 years old, stands 11 meters tall, and has an impressive basal perimeter of 7.40 meters. It's also part of the 'Ruta de las Catedrales Vivas' (Route of the Living Cathedrals), highlighting its ecological and cultural importance.
